screenSummary.playArea: The play area shows a particle source wired through Stern–Gerlach analyzers and magnets to particle counters on a light experiment board. Animated magenta dots represent atoms in flight. In Custom mode you can add devices, drag them into place, and wire output ports to input ports.
screenSummary.controlArea: The control area has an experiment preset chooser, a quantum-system selector, an initial-state chooser, watch and expected-value toggles, direction-angle controls, analytic Do-N batch buttons, and Reset Counts. Reset All returns the simulation to its starting state.
screenSummary.interactionHint: Choose an experiment and system, pick an initial state, then fire atoms through the analyzers and compare counter statistics with the expected values.

a11y.currentDetailsPattern: {{experiment}}, {{system}} system. Initial state: {{initialState}}. Atoms detected: {{total}}. Watch is {{watchStatus}}.

controls.expectedValuesCheckbox: Show expected-value lines on the counters
controls.watchCheckbox: Watch which path each atom takes

controls.counterToolboxItem: Add a counter to the board
controls.userStateBasisRadioGroup: Input basis for the user-defined state

keyboardHelp.fireAtom: Fire one atom from the source
keyboardHelp.moveDevice: Move a device on the board
keyboardHelp.dragWire: Drag from an output port to an input port

preferences.title: Simulation
preferences.su3EnableDescription: Adds SU(3) (Gell-Mann λ₁–λ₈) as a third quantum system.
